title: Leafs want Gudas punished more for Matthews hit
content: The Maple Leafs believe the NHL didn't go far enough in punishing Ducks defenseman Radko Gudas for the hit that ended Auston Matthews' season.

title: Moser returning to OU with player NIL upgrade
content: Oklahoma men's basketball coach Porter Moser will return for the 2026-27 season, athletic director Roger Denny told ESPN on Saturday.

title: Langeliers homers 3 times in spring training game
content: Athletics catcher Shea Langeliers homered in all three of his three plate appearances in a spring training game Saturday against the Royals.

title: Irsay collection nets more than $93M at auction
content: An expansive memorabilia collection belonging to late Colts owner Jim Irsay has brought in more than $93 million via auction with Christie's.

title: F1 cancels Bahrain, Saudi races due to Iran war
content: Formula 1 has confirmed that the grands prix in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia will be canceled due to the war in Iran.

title: UNC, WVU make women's top 16, to host tourney
content: The top 16 seeds of the women's NCAA tournament were announced Saturday, with both North Carolina and West Virginia making the cut.

title: Party foul: Red flag for caterers on IndyCar track
content: IndyCar officials had to throw a red flag to slow the cars as they were pulling out of the pit area during practice when three workers were spotted on the hot track.

title: 🏈 Which NFL teams have improved most in FA?
content: We picked out the teams that took the biggest steps forward this week through their signings and other moves -- including the 49ers.

title: Men's Bracketology: What the SEC's bloated bubble means for the overall field
content: The conference could finish with anywhere from nine to 12 bids with Texas, Oklahoma and Auburn in question.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: What VCU's win, Saint Louis' loss mean for the A-10 picture
content: The Rams advance to the Atlantic 10 final, potentially controlling more than just their NCAA tournament fate.

title: Women's Bracketology: How the committee's top-16 reveal rippled through the field
content: Saturday, the committee revealed the top-16 teams that will host early round games. North Carolina moved in.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: From Team Japan to MLB stars: Who could be next to make the jump?
content: The World Baseball Classic has given fans a chance to see Japan's best players before they hit the majors. Here's who to keep an eye on this year.

title: Arteta turned to the teenage Dowman, and it paid off tremendously for Arsenal
content: It was a night where Max Dowman made history for Arsenal, and may have helped them go in touching distance of the Premier League title.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: New deal by Monday is goal as marathon meeting continues
content: The WNBA and union exchanged about 15 proposals between Tuesday and the early hours of Saturday morning.

title: NBA draft intel and latest buzz: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1? What's Boozer's case?
content: Here's what league insiders are saying about Darryn Peterson, AJ Dybantsa and more top NBA draft prospects.

title: 'I want to earn it': Tennessee track lands commit from Kevin Hart's son Hendrix
content: Hendrix Hart's personal bests include 10.68 seconds in the 100-meter dash and 21.35 in the 200-meter dash.

title: Men's coaching tracker: Who could be up next at Cincinnati?
content: The Bearcats are moving on from Wes Miller after five seasons -- but have promising candidates on deck.

title: Full 2026 draft order: When teams will pick across all 257 selections, seven rounds
content: The order for all seven rounds of the 2026 NFL draft is set, starting with the Raiders at No. 1 and ending with the Broncos at No. 257.

title: Verstappen: Every lap in Red Bull is survival mode
content: Max Verstappen said every lap is survival mode with his Red Bull at the moment as he cannot control the car, a situation that is "just really not how it should be."

title: Weekend predictions: Can Spurs stop record losing streak at Liverpool?
content: Another weekend of action awaits across the soccer calendar as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe. Will Spurs sink deeper into the relegation battle?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United eye move for USMNT's Adams
content: Manchester United are reported to be leading the way to sign Bournemouth and USMNT midfielder Tyler Adams. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Risers and fallers: Filipowski, da Silva moving up; Flagg a problem during playoffs?
content: Tristan da Silva is on the rise, and should fantasy basketball managers keep players like Bennedict Mathurin and Cameron Johnson?

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: 'Must take care': Trump urges nations to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
content: Former US President Donald Trump urged global nations to deploy warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz amidst Iranian retaliation. He stated the US would assist in coordinating efforts to ensure smooth passage for oil shipments. Trump also indicated the US would take strong action against Iranian vessels.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: Inside Iran’s ‘Mosaic defence’: Why killing top commanders may not break Tehran’s war machine
content: Iran's "mosaic defence" strategy ensures the nation can fight on even after devastating attacks. This decentralized model distributes command across multiple nodes, making it resilient to leadership decapitation and infrastructure destruction.  It aims to prolong conflicts, turning them into exhausting wars of attrition rather than quick, decisive campaigns, a lesson learned from past regional conflicts.

title: Cognizant advises BYOF for employees, reviews supply concerns
content: Cognizant is encouraging employees to bring their own food as part of contingency planning amid the West Asia crisis. The IT major is preparing for potential disruptions in food services and transport due to energy market volatility. The company is exploring alternative vendors and energy sources to mitigate risks.

title: 'Incredibly difficult': Atlassian CEO on decision of laying off 1,600 engineers
content: Atlassian has laid off approximately 1,600 employees, about 10% of its global workforce, to invest more in AI and enterprise sales. The cuts primarily affected R&D roles.  CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes highlighted the difficult decision, emphasizing the need for competitiveness.  The company is offering generous severance packages, with redundancy and office exit costs estimated at over $236 million.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Bo Nix’s Denver Broncos tied to $31M Washington Commanders WR
content: Deebo Samuel’s long-held desire to play for the Denver Broncos might finally materialize this season. The versatile wide receiver is known for his dual-threat abilities. He is seen as a perfect fit for Sean Payton’s scheme of playing. Despite injury concerns, Samuel’s potential impact on the gridiron, coupled with Denver’s need for offensive play, makes this an irresistible prospect for the franchise. Especially where Bo Nix’s style of playing is concerned. A running back and wideout in one player could benefit Nix.

title: Sidney Crosby injury update after Mitch Marner reveals Olympics setback
content: The Pittsburgh Penguins are still tracking the progress of captain Sidney Crosby, who sustained injuries at the 2026 winter Olympics. Recent news by Vegas Golden Knights forward Mitch Marner has given insight into the gravity of the problem. Marner reported that Crosby was sprained with Grade 2 of an MCL, which means that the Penguins awaited the return of the captain.

title: Fortnite Story Moment Power Hour event (March 14, 2026): Start timings for all regions
content: Fortnite's "Story Moment Power Hour" is scheduled for March 14, 2026, bridging the gap between Chapter 7 Season 1 and Season 2. This unrepeatable, live event will feature The Dark Voyager and provide crucial narrative context for the "Showdown." Players must log in precisely at their region's start time to participate.

title: India’s Goldilocks under threat? How war, crude oil above $100 may deal a blow to growth story
content: What do rising crude oil prices spell for India’s GDP growth, inflation, current account deficit, rupee, and fiscal deficit? Economists say that the answer lies in the length of the war.

title: Indian women's team qualify for Hockey World Cup despite runners-up finish
content: India’s women’s hockey team finished runners-up in the FIH Hockey World Cup Qualifiers after a 0–2 loss to England but still qualified for the World Cup. Grace Balsdon scored from a penalty corner in the 13th minute, while Elizabeth Neal added another in the 43rd. India created chances but couldn’t score. England, India and Scotland qualified for the tournament.

title: Middle East crisis Day 15: Iran hits US embassy in Baghdad, UAE oil hub struck
content: The US-Israel conflict with Iran escalated significantly on its 15th day. Iran targeted the US embassy in Baghdad and a UAE oil hub, while Israel struck Lebanon and Iraq. Debris from intercepted missiles hit Dubai twice. The war now directly impacts diplomatic sites and oil infrastructure across the Gulf, with UAE intercepting over 1,800 Iranian projectiles.

title: An unholy havoc? Swami Avimukteshwaranand & his coronation with controversy
content: In recent weeks, the seer has found himself at the centre of a political and legal storm that stretches from allegations under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act to a decades old dispute over who has the right to call himself the Shankaracharya of Jyotirmath. For many followers of Sanatan Dharm, the position of Shankaracharya is among the most revered spiritual roles in Hindu tradition.

title: Kuldeep Yadav ties knot with Vanshika Chadha - pics out
content: Kuldeep Yadav married Vanshika Chadha in a ceremony in Mussoorie, Uttarakhand, days after India’s ICC Men's T20 World Cup triumph. Several cricketers attended the celebrations, with a grand reception planned in Lucknow. Teammates including Yuzvendra Chahal and Tilak Varma expressed excitement while enjoying the wedding festivities before returning to cricket commitments.

title: Golmaal 5 first look: Akshay Kumar’s avatar steals show in Ajay Devgn film
content: Filmmaker Rohit Shetty has announced Golmaal 5, marking the franchise's return with Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, and others. A major surprise is Akshay Kumar joining the cast, sporting a new bald look.  Shetty expressed gratitude to fans for two decades of support, promising more entertainment as filming commences for this highly anticipated comedy.

title: "Thanked Great Friend PM Modi For Standing With Israel": Netanyahu To NDTV
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu today told NDTV he spoke to his "great friend" Prime Minister Narendra Modi and thanked him for standing with Israel amid tensions in the Middle East.

title: 'We're Hitting Back Very Hard': Top Israel General To NDTV On Hezbollah
content: Brigadier General Effie Defrin, spokesperson for the Israel Defence Forces (IDF), told NDTV that Hezbollah's decision to resume fire roughly 10 days ago set off the current escalation, and that Israel had no intention of stopping.

title: 'Send Warships': Trump's Message To World To End Iran's Hormuz Chokehold
content: As the war with Iran entered its 15th day, US President Donald Trump urged other nations to send ships to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US-Israel-Iran War LIVE: Iran's Foreign Minister Says 'No Problem' With Supreme Leader Mojtaba After Injury Reports
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Iran's Army chief has issued a stern warning that the deaths of crew members from the IRIS Dena warship in the Indian Ocean "will not go unanswered", as reported by Al Jazeera, citing Iran's state news agency.

title: Congress Releases Second List Of 23 Candidates For Assam Polls
content: The Congress on Saturday released its second list of 23 party candidates for the upcoming Assam assembly elections. It has also left 15 seats for its alliance partners in the northeastern state, where assembly elections are slated in April.

title: Man Kills Daughter, Her Lover In Gujarat. He Didn't Approve The Relationship
content: A couple from Gujarat who had been missing for over a month was found dead in a well in Khambhala village of Rajkot district.

title: 100 Stray Dogs "Poisoned To Death" In Telangana: Police
content: In yet another incident of stray dog killings, nearly 100 canines were allegedly "poisoned to death" in Mancherial district, police said.

title: "Act Of Aggression": India Slams Pak's Deadly Air Strikes In Afghanistan
content: Terming it as another act of aggression by a Pakistani establishment that remains hostile to the idea of a sovereign Afghanistan, India on Saturday severely condemned the latest strikes by Pakistan inside Afghan territory.

title: 'Legitimate Targets': Iran Asks People To Leave Port Areas In UAE
content: Smoke was seen rising from the direction of Fujairah's energy installations today as Iran warned residents of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) to immediately evacuate areas around three of the country's major ports.

title: PM Modi Inaugurates APSEZ's Haldia Bulk Terminal
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday commissioned the Haldia Bulk Terminal (HBT) virtually from Kolkata's iconic Brigade parade ground. Haldia Bulk Terminal is designed to handle dry bulk cargo at an annual capacity of 4 MMTPA.

title: Iran May Allow Oil Tankers Through Strait Of Hormuz, But There's A China Catch
content: Iran is reportedly considering a new approach to manage oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical energy routes.

title: Pipes vs Cylinders: India's PNG Advantage And LPG Concern Explained
content: For many Indians, LPG cylinders have been the most common cooking fuel for years. However, PNG or Piped Natural Gas is gradually becoming a popular alternative across the country.

title: PM Modi Inaugurates APSEZ's Haldia Bulk Terminal In Ahmedabad
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday commissioned the Haldia Bulk Terminal (HBT) and formally dedicated it to the nation.

title: Video: US Hammers Iran's Kharg Island, Over 90 Targets Destroyed In Strikes
content: The United States forces executed a large-scale precision strike on Iran's Kharg Island, its biggest oil export hub, on Friday night, the US Central Command said on Saturday.

title: "Orphan Pearl" On Fire: The Critical Oil Island Struck By US In Iran
content: US Strikes Iran: Spread over a meagre 20 square kilometres, Iran's Kharg island processes 90 per cent of the country's total oil exports, driving the region to the edge as the US attacked it on Friday, 15 days into the Middle East conflict.

title: Bodycam Shows Israeli Troops Raid Beirut, South Lebanon In Night Missions
content: The Israel Defence Forces (IDF) on Thursday released body-cam footage showing an operation targeting sites that they alleged were linked to the militant group Hezbollah in Beirut and southern Lebanon.

title: Major UAE Oil Terminal In Flames Hours After US Strikes Iran's Kharg Island
content: Dark smoke was seen rising on Saturday from a major energy installation in the United Arab Emirates. This comes hours after the United States carried out strikes on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub.

title: 'India Has Adequate Fertiliser Stocks': Government Sources Amid Iran's Hormuz Chokehold
content: The Iran conflict has disrupted supply routes through the Strait of Hormuz, a key corridor for global fertiliser shipments, pushing up prices of crop nutrients such as urea and ammonia and raising import costs for major buyers like India.

title: 'It Was Suicide': Bengaluru Man Told Lover's Parents. Then, A Murder Twist
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her live-in partner and it was made to look like a suicide in Bengaluru's Varthur police limits.

title: Tamil Lyricist Vairamuthu Named 2025 Jnanpith Award Winner
content: The 60th Jnanpith Award, India's highest literary honour, for the year 2025 will be conferred on distinguished Tamil poet, lyricist and writer R. Vairamuthu.

title: Watch: MS Dhoni, Ruturaj Gaikwad blast bowlers; video goes viral ahead of IPL 2026
content: Chennai Super Kings have begun preparations for the Indian Premier League 2026 season, with a viral video showing MS Dhoni and captain Ruturaj Gaikwad hitting big shots in the nets. After missing the playoffs recently, CSK aim for a sixth title. They start their campaign against Rajasthan Royals on March 30.

title: Paulina Gretzky gets white rose bouquet from husband Dustin Johnson
content: Paulina Gretzky has just shown a piece of a romantic gift her husband, professional golf player Dustin Johnson, gave her. Wayne Gretzky, an NHL legend, entered a photo of a huge bunch of white roses with a sweet note on it, posted by his daughter. The move soon attracted the interest of fans, and it portrays a lifetime relationship and family life togetherness of the couple.

title: Pak employees face heat: Up to 30% salary cuts in state firms amid fuel crisis
content: Pakistan's Prime Minister has approved significant salary cuts for state-owned enterprise employees, ranging from 5 to 30 percent, as part of a broad austerity drive. This move follows a sharp fuel price hike linked to the Middle East conflict.  The government is also reducing fuel allocations for official vehicles and banning new purchases to fund public relief efforts.

title: F1 WAGs Carmen Montero Mundt, Alexandra Leclerc spotted in Shanghai
content: George Russell's girlfriend Carmen Montero Mundt and Charles Leclerc's girlfriend Alexandra Leclerc bonded off the track as Russell won the Sprint and finished second in the Chinese Grand Prix qualifying. Leclerc finished fourth in the qualifying. The Chinese Grand Prix final will take place on Sunday and Kimi Antonelli will start from the pole position.

title: Cian Shields to make Formula E debut with Cupra Kiro at Madrid Rookie Test
content: Formula 2 racer Cian Shields is all set to make his Formula E debut with Cupra Kiro at the upcoming Madrid Rookie Test. The 21-year-old will be joining Cupra Kiro development driver Biance Bustamante at the test. It will take place at the Circuito del Jarama in Madrid on March 22.

title: 'That's all you need': Ponting reveals 'big decision' behind India's T20 WC title
content: Former Australia captain Ricky Ponting praised India’s decision to back Sanju Samson as opener in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. Samson recovered from a slow start to score 321 runs, including key knocks against the West Indies national cricket team, England national cricket team and New Zealand national cricket team. Ponting credited captain Suryakumar Yadav and the team management for showing faith in him and guiding India to the title.

title: 'Yet another act of aggression': India condemns Pakistan air strikes in Afghanistan
content: India has strongly condemned Pakistan's airstrikes in Afghanistan, labeling them an "act of aggression" and emphasizing the need to respect Afghanistan's sovereignty. The strikes, which reportedly caused civilian casualties, follow accusations by the Afghan Taliban government against Pakistan. Pakistan claims its operations target Pakistani Taliban militants and their support networks.

title: 'I can't': England legend Kevin Pietersen quits Delhi Capitals role - here's why
content: Former England cricketer Kevin Pietersen has stepped down as mentor of Delhi Capitals ahead of the 2026 Indian Premier League, citing lack of time for the role. He will still appear as a commentator during the tournament. Pietersen joined the franchise in 2025 and previously played for several IPL teams during his career. The season begins March 28 in Bengaluru.

title: Iran crisis: MEA says five Indians killed, one missing in conflict
content: Tragedy strikes as five Indians lose their lives and one remains missing amid escalating Middle East conflict. Meanwhile, two Indian LPG carriers have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, en route to India with vital fuel supplies. Missions are working tirelessly to aid affected nationals and ensure repatriation.

title: Kuldeep Yadav wedding: When and where is the ceremony? All you need to know
content: Indian spinner Kuldeep Yadav is set to tie the knot with Vanshika Chadha on March 14, 2026, in Mussoorie, following India's T20 World Cup victory. Pre-wedding rituals are underway at the historic Savoy Hotel. A grand reception in Lucknow on March 17 will host prominent figures from cricket, politics, and business.

title: Punjab: Shah rules out Akali alliance revival, says BJP will fight 2027 polls to form own govt
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah declared the BJP will contest the 2027 Punjab Assembly elections independently, aiming to form its own government. He asserted the party's growing vote share and criticized the AAP government's performance on law and order, drugs, and corruption. Shah also pledged to enact a law against religious conversions if the BJP wins.

title: Ranveer Allahbadia says Karan Johar first to call after ‘India’s Got Latent’ row
content: YouTuber Ranveer Allahbadia revealed filmmaker Karan Johar offered support during his 2025 controversy on 'India's Got Latent'. Johar advised him to focus on mental health, which proved crucial. Allahbadia shared the incident deeply affected him and his mother, who was physically attacked, highlighting the trauma of online outrage and cancel culture.

title: US shares video of strikes on 90 Iranian military targets on Kharg Island - watch
content: United States President Donald Trump said American forces carried out “the most powerful bombing raid” on Iran’s Kharg Island, claiming that American forces “totally obliterated” military targets there while choosing not to destroy the island’s oil infrastructure. ​​Any disruption at Kharg Island can significantly affect global oil supply routes, making it a critical node for global energy security and oil market stability.

title: ‘Pandit’ option for question on opportunists trips up BJP in U.P. days after ‘anti-Brahmin’ claim by opposition
content: Uttar Pradesh BJP secretary Abhijat Mishra, who belongs to the Brahmin community, has written to Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath, raising objections to the question and seeking action against those responsible for setting the paper

title: Close fight on the cards in Kerala’s bellwether constituency — Ollur
content: Since 1982, Ollur has elected candidates from the front that eventually forms the government in the State. Urban-rural mix, strong religious influence, and shifting voter loyalties make the constituency politically complex

title: Jeweller arrested in IDFC First Bank fraud case in Haryana; 12th arrest
content: The case pertains to private lender IDFC First Bank, which had disclosed a ₹590 crore fraud involving certain employees and others at its Chandigarh branch in a set of accounts held by the Haryana government

title: Cong releases second list of 23 candidates for Assam polls
content: Congress general secretary (organisation) K.C. Venugopal released the second list of candidates for the 126-member Assembly; the party also left 15 seats for its alliance partners

title: Why did the SC allow passive euthanasia? | Explained
content: What test did the Supreme Court apply in the Harish Rana case to decide if continuing treatment was in his best interest? What nuanced distinction did it provide between active and passive euthanasia? What have been India’s past positions on the right to die?

title: Mojtaba Khamenei | The cleric with a gun
content: A wounded man who lost his parents and wife, and was elected the ‘Supreme Leader’ of a nation under attack from the world’s most powerful country and its closest ally, the ayatollah now faces a rare crisis his predecessors never confronted — to survive the storm and preserve the republic

title: Experts laud A.P. CM’s vision for Quantum Valley project
content: The project envisions the use of real, cutting-edge quantum science to position Andhra Pradesh as a global leader in the technology, they say

title: Amma canteens in Chennai see an increase in sales as LPG crisis forces hotels to shut
content: Corporation officials said adequate stocks of LPG cylinders will be made available for Amma Canteens to facilitate an increase in the quantity of food cooked

title: Corporation mulling community kitchens to ensure food: Mayor
content: He adds that the Corporation will intervene strongly to check hoarding and black marketeering of LPG cylinders; hotel owners directed not to fleece consumers in the name of LPG shortage

title: CMC organises competitions for school students to mark World Diabetes Day
content: CMC aims to highlight the importance of early detection, optimal glycemic control, lifestyle modification, and psychological well-being in preventing long-term neurological complications

title: Man held for possession of 300 kg gutka near Katpadi
content: Arsuram was coming from Bengaluru to deliver the consignment to traders in Vellore and adjoining towns

title: Deputy CM Udhayanidhi Stalin distributes welfare aid worth ₹100.49 crore in Tirupattur
content: After inaugurating 11 completed works worth ₹3.65 crore, Mr. Udhayanidhi says achieving zero percent in school dropout rates in the State is among the priorities of the Dravidian Model 2.0 government

title: CM Stalin writes to PM to increase rice procurement
content: In a letter to Mr. Modi, he said the timely opening of the Mettur Dam for irrigation, favourable climatic conditions, increased area coverage, improved crop management practices, better irrigation support, and the adoption of high-yielding varieties of paddy by farmers

title: International status for Madurai airport will draw more tourists, pilgrims, says Union Minister
content: Formally according the status to Madurai airport, Civil Aviation Minister Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu says the move will also help create more jobs and greatly boost hospitality, MSME, and agricultural sectors in the district; he also inaugurates new air traffic control tower set up at cost of ₹94 crore

title: Varsity created needless controversy, says renowned feminist historian Uma Chakravarti
content: ‘The historical roots of patriarchy need to be understood. We need to study as well, because we have to substantiate our point of view. Changes have happened when we have understood changes,’ she said

title: Roadside eateries in Vijayawada worst hit by LPG crisis
content: Street vendors and owners of carts are struggling to keep their stalls running, while several others have either cut down their operations or closed temporarily

title: India at a crossroads, next step to define country’s future: Kamal Haasan
content: At the book launch of author and former West Bengal Governor Gopalkrishna Gandhi’s India and Her Futures, actor-MP Kamal Haasan said the question before our country is whether we join the chorus of nations exporting division as politics or remain true to the extraordinary, ethical, and moral imagination that created India

title: Transcultural exchanges have long shaped Kannada literature: Shivanand Kelaginamani
content: The professor says the long history of the Kannada language can be traced through classical works such as Kavirajamarga; he notes that elements from Sanskrit were gradually incorporated into Kannada.

title: India facilitating negotiations within BRICS on West Asia conflict: Jaiswal
content: Acknowledgement of intra-BRICS negotiation comes even as External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar continues his telephone calls with his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi and other regional diplomats

title: Why Indian airlines are among the worst hit by the Gulf conflict
content: Almost four crore people flew between India and the Gulf in 2025; half of all international passengers flying to or from India was on a Gulf route; idle aircraft, longer flights, higher costs, lower revenue all hurting Indian carriers

title: Kalaburagi Rangayana to organise month-long summer theatre workshop for children
content: Rangayana director Sujata Jangamshetty said the workshop aims to help children explore their creative abilities, understand theatre art and improve their personality development.

title: Ladakh to protest for safeguards on March 16, despite Sonam Wangchuk release
content: Struggle for rights continue, say civil society groups while demanding release of two more detained since September 2025; Opposition questions Centre over the prolonged detention of the activist and says PM should apologise to people of Ladakh

title: LPG crunch puts strain on mid-day meals for children at schools across India
content: Many schools are cutting down on their daily menu, affecting the nutritional quality of the food being provided to the children; reports indicate that as schools apply for gas cylinder refills, the situation may become dicey

title: Chief Minister M.K. Stalin meets Deva and Jiva, the child influencers who tell everyone to study
content: The brothers, natives of Sathipattu near Panruti in Cuddalore district, are known figures on social media through their reels over the last one-and-a-half years

title: Coffee-table book on wildlife of Tamil Nadu launched
content: The book, published jointly by The Hindu Group and the Department of Tourism, presents a visual and narrative journey through the State’s diverse natural landscapes, ecological systems, and cultural history

title: SC seeks status report on Ganga bank encroachments
content: Court asks Centre and National Mission for Ganga Authority to detail action on encroachments and floodplain demarcation along River Ganga

title: 23 students injured as goods train rams college bus in Karur
content: The accident occurred on a private railway line owned and operated by Chettinad Cement between Veerarakkiyam and Palayam

title: ‘Bengaluru’s wet waste has potential to meet 20% of gas consumed by restaurants’
content: The proposal was made by Som Narayan, co-founder and director of Carbon Masters, which operates a biomethanisation plant in Koramangala

title: Government bars PNG users from retaining or refilling LPG connections
content: Order aimed at protecting domestic LPG supply as West Asia tensions disrupt imports through Strait of Hormuz

title: Commercial LPG supplies in Telangana meeting only 20% demand; Uttam urges Centre for more commercial cylinders
content: Civil Supplies Minister writes to Centre requesting additional commercial LPG cylinders; appeals to people not to overbook domestic refills as contingency measures have been initiated

title: Seven-member SIT to investigate assassination attempt on Farooq Abdullah
content: The SIT has been formed after the former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister expressed concern over the security arrangements at the venue where attempt took place on March 11

title: As Samriddhi Yatra ends in Bihar, Nitish promises more development
content: Referring to the Opposition Rashtriya Janata Dal as ‘useless people’, the Bihar CM reiterates that he left the alliance with them following their wrongdoings within the government

title: Emotional farewell for KSCDC chairperson S. Jayamohan
content: The outgoing chairman leaves a decade-long tenure marked by a remarkable turnaround of the crisis-ridden PSU

title: Menstrual leave policy at national level keeping interest of women in mind would be welcome: Karnataka’s Labour Minister Santosh Lad
content: Karnataka has framed Karnataka Women Well Being Leave Bill, 2025, which is yet to be tabled in the Legislatur

title: Karnataka prisons to introduce drug tests for inmates to curb smuggling of narcotic substances
content: Under the initiative, inmates will be tested for substances such as ganja, heroin and cocaine. In the first phase, a group  of prisoners in all jails will undergo screening

title: Immensely pleased to celebrate JSP Formation Day in remote tribal area, says Pawan Kalyan
content: He unfurls the party flag in the presence of tribal communities at Onuru Junction and later walks  for about 2.4 km to inspect the road laid under the PM JANMAN scheme

title: Our emphasis is to promote raw talent, Union Sports Minister Mandaviya
content: The Union Minister says that the sports sector has opened up umpteen opportunities for youngsters to build their careers in the field.

title: Dirty water flows into Mahamagam Tank in Kumbakonam; officials swing into action
content: A clip showing black water flowing into the tank goes viral; MLA says stagnant water in Arasalaru flowed into the tank; civic officials spray disinfectants to make it safe

title: Hotel industry in Kerala fears migrant exodus amid LPG crisis
content: ‘Migrants dominate the workforce in hotels as local residents are virtually absent from the industry. Shutting down hotels risks not only losing customers and revenue but also workers, who may choose to return home’

title: 460-km roads laid in tribal areas in last 18 months, says Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan
content: The coalition government in the State is a people-friendly administration that is committed to addressing public grievances, he says

title: Four sitting MPs’ keenness to contest Assembly polls puts Congress in a spot in Kerala
content: Party’s central leadership had repeatedly asked sitting MPs not to contest Assembly polls; unrest in party’s Pathanamthitta unit over alleged move to field candidates from even outside the district

title: Over 2.95 lakh cases settled in National Lok Adalat across Andhra Pradesh
content: Judicial officers order payment of ₹87.35 crore compensation as 400 Lok Adalat benches dispose of pending and pre-litigation cases across the State

title: Catering industry takes a hit amid LPG cylinder shortage
content: Caterers in Thiruvananthapuram stop taking fresh orders as cylinder shortage looms large; some switch to firewood, but point out its inconvenience

title: ASI's Bhojshala report hearing in HC tomorrow
content:
.

INDORE: Madhya Pradesh high court will hear on Monday the objections and suggestions to the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) report on its survey of disputed Bhojshala-Kamal Maula Mosque complex, which concludes that the mosque was constructed using parts of ancient temples. "Based on scientific investigations, surveys and archaeological excavations, study and analysis of retrieved finds, and examination of architectural remains, sculptures, inscriptions and art, it can be said that the existing structure was made from parts of earlier temples," says the ASI report, spanning 10 volumes and over 2,000 pages. The HC's Indore bench was hearing the petitions on the directions of Supreme Court.

End of Article

title: Arrest Badshah: Haryana women's panel to police
content:
.

CHANDIGARH: Haryana State Commission for Women on Saturday directed police to arrest rapper Badshah alias Aditya Prateek Singh Sisodia in cases registered against him at three places over his controversial song 'Tateeree'. One of the three is a criminal case. HSCW chairperson Renu Bhatia issued directions to Panchkula DC and Panipat SP, where the cases are registered, for "execution of orders as soon as possible". She also directed police and authorities to seize Badshah's passport and ban his programmes across the state. "Despite our reminder, he did not turn up for the hearing and instead released another video message. We gave him an opportunity till 3pm, as his lawyers pleaded that he could not come for the hearing due to professional commitments. This was not acceptable, hence, we issued the directions," said Bhatia. t

End of Article
